idea.title: Token Throttle
idea.hook: You're a dispatcher routing tasks to cheap or expensive AI agents — pick wrong and your monthly budget burns before quota refills.
idea.hook_ru: Ты диспетчер, раздающий задачи дешёвым или дорогим ИИ-агентам — ошибся, и месячный бюджет сгорит до пополнения квоты.
idea.prototype: 2D canvas dashboard. Tasks queue from the left (rename var / refactor / design doc / unit test, each tagged with complexity 1-5). Assign each to one of 3 agents: Haiku ($, fast, fails on >3 complexity), Sonnet ($$, reliable, fails on >4), Opus ($$$$, perfect but bleeds budget). Wrong-tier assignments cause rework loops (task re-enters queue +1 complexity). Two-tap routing: tap task to select, tap agent to dispatch. Keyboard 1/2/3 also routes the leftmost task. Score = tasks shipped before budget zeroes. Lose when budget < cheapest agent's per-task cost.
idea.why_novel: First mechanic to make COMPLEXITY a routing variable with a rework-loop PENALTY rather than a one-shot lane sort. Every wrong dispatch makes the SAME task more expensive next time, creating compounding-cost decisions completely distinct from 56_token_triage's escape-conveyor pressure or 32_token_budget's static allocation. The complexity dots on every card mean reading is part of the input, not just routing.
idea.non_timer_pressure: Token wallet (60 → 0). No clock. Every dispatch drains tokens by agent cost; mis-routes loop the task back at +1 complexity which then needs a more expensive agent. Lose the moment budget < CHEAPEST_COST (=1).
idea.lose_condition: budget < CHEAPEST_COST (= Haiku's 1 token) — you cannot afford even the cheapest dispatch.

sources.title: Token Throttle
sources.snippet: Entry #3 — Token Throttle: dispatcher routing tasks to cheap or expensive AI agents — pick wrong and your monthly budget burns before quota refills. Distinct from Token Triage / Token Budget — queue-routing dispatcher with rework feedback loop.

sources.title: Opus 4.7 just to rename a variable
sources.snippet: Vibe-coder humor about burning Opus tokens on trivial work — the exact tier-mismatch pain Token Throttle gamifies.

sources.title: Agent cost economics matters more than raw model quality
sources.snippet: Reserve expensive models for judgement, use cheaper agents for repetitive automation — the routing rule the game tests you on.

sources.title: Agent cost theme
sources.snippet: Background scripts and automated tools now bleed budget — the dispatcher-on-finite-wallet frame is right there.

synthesizer_brief: Dispatcher arcade where you tap a complexity-tagged task card then tap one of three AI agents (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus) with tiered cost AND tiered complexity ceilings. Wrong-tier dispatch loops the task back with complexity +1, making the next routing more expensive. Endless score-attack on a 60-token wallet; lose when budget < cheapest agent cost. Distinct from 32_token_budget (static allocation, no rework loop) and 56_token_triage (conveyor + escape pressure, no complexity-bump penalty). The novelty is the rework-loop snowball — every cheap shortcut compounds into expensive future rework. Source signals from vibecoding_tg/3154 + 3153 + spacewolfblog/12006 give the agent-cost framing real cultural traction.

alternatives.title: Ice Fracture Foreman
alternatives.hook: Tap a frozen lake to crack it in branching fissures, isolating fish into a shrinking pond before the cracks reach your hut.
alternatives.why_novel: Hex-tile crack propagation + corral spatial puzzle

alternatives.title: Shader Saturday
alternatives.hook: Drag the post-process slider chain to match the day's screenshot — wrong order makes your render look like AI slop and judges roast you.
alternatives.why_novel: Effect-block reorder puzzle with fan-retention life pool

alternatives.title: Tier-1 Pitch
alternatives.hook: Drag elements onto your store page — title, hook, icon, screenshot — and watch English-speaking testers either click Wishlist or bounce in disgust.
alternatives.why_novel: Asset-drag pitch builder with simulated Tier-1 tester reactions

alternatives.title: Tiny Markov Dunge
alternatives.hook: A 200-byte room-generator paints your dungeon in real time — your only weapon is editing the seed before the boss spawns from your own typo.
alternatives.why_novel: Seed-edit ink as the only weapon vs Markov-chain layout generator
